source.page_label: Turkey Dates, Rules & Regulations | Arkansas Game & Fish Commission
source.update_note: 2026 upland game bird data from AGFC turkey and quail regulation pages. Arkansas's upland game birds are primarily bobwhite quail and wild turkey. Turkey seasons are zone-based with spring and fall components.

species.name: Wild Turkey (Spring)
species.season_raw: Zone 1: Apr 20-May 10; Zone 1A: Apr 20-28; Zone 2: Apr 13-May 3; Zone 2A: Apr 13-21; Zone 3: Apr 6-26
species.bag_limit: 2 legal turkeys per season (1 per day)

sub_seasons.name: Zone 1
sub_seasons.note: Second bird must be taken in NWR, WMA, or Zones 1, 2, 2A, or 3

sub_seasons.name: Zone 1A
sub_seasons.note: Second bird must be taken in NWR, WMA, or Zones 1, 2, 2A, or 3

sub_seasons.name: Zone 2A
sub_seasons.note: Second bird must be taken in NWR, WMA, or Zones 1, 1A, 2, or 3

licenses.name: Resident Wildlife Conservation License (HNT)
licenses.covers: Small game, furbearers, and one deer; base hunting license

purchase_urls.label: Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal

licenses.name: Resident Sportsman's License (RS)
licenses.covers: All game (6 deer tags, 2 turkey tags), hunting only

purchase_urls.label: Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal
Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal

licenses.name: Nonresident 5-Day Small Game License (SG5)
licenses.covers: Small game and furbearers for 5 consecutive days

purchase_urls.label: Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal
Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal

licenses.name: Nonresident Turkey Tag (NRTT)
licenses.covers: Required for NBG holders to take one turkey

purchase_urls.label: Arkansas Game & Fish — Online License Portal

key_notes.title: Arkansas turkey hunters may take 2 legal turkeys per spring season. No jakes or hens may be harvested. Daily limit is 1 legal turkey.

sources.label: AGFC — Turkey Dates, Rules & Regulations
sources.evidence: Total limit: 2 legal turkeys (no jakes, no females). First 7 days of regular season: no more than one legal turkey may be taken. Daily limit: One legal turkey per day.

key_notes.title: Youth turkey hunt (ages 6-15) is held April 11-12, 2026 in Zones 1, 1A, 2, and 2A. Youth without hunter education must be supervised by an adult 21 or older.

sources.label: AGFC — Special Youth Turkey Hunt
sources.evidence: 2026 Special Youth Turkey Hunt: April 11-12, 2026 (Zones 1, 1A, 2, and 2A only). Hunters ages 6-15. Bag limit: One legal turkey or jake.

key_notes.title: Shot larger than No. 2 common shot is prohibited for turkey hunting. Legal weapons: shotguns (10 gauge or smaller) and archery equipment.

sources.label: AGFC — Turkey Hunting Regulations
sources.evidence: Shot larger than No. 2 common shot is prohibited. Legal weapons: shotguns (10 gauge or smaller) and archery equipment (including crossbows) only.

key_notes.title: Nonresident turkey hunters may only take one turkey per season. A nonresident turkey tag ($100) is required in addition to the hunting license.

sources.label: AGFC — License Descriptions and Fees
sources.evidence: Nonresidents (youth and adult): Only one turkey in seasonal bag limit. Nonresident Turkey Tag (NRTT): $100. Required for holders of NBG to take one turkey.
